Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 1, 011 [Enuma Eliš 7][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
oo NaN  (start of obverse missing)
o 1'o 1'

[... ma]-⸢a⸣-[ti]

[...] the land,

o 2'2'

[...] ⸢nu⸣-uḫ-⸢ši⸣

[...] abundance.

o 3'3'

[...] x x [...] x ⸢KAR!(A:TE)e ša₂-niš iz-zak-⸢ru⸣

They shall invoke him secondly [...] ... [...] ... desolation,

o 4'4'

[...] ⸢KI⸣ti₃ mu-kin-nu AB.⸢SIN₂⸣

[...] Underworld, establisher of the furrow,

o 5'5'

[...] u₂-kin-nu ina ṣe-e-ri

[...] established [...] in the open country,

o 6'6'

[... pal]-⸢ka uš⸣-te-eš-še-ru ⸢uṣ⸣-ṣi-ru ap-ki-sa

who keeps [...] ditch in order (and) planned the furrow.

o 7'7'

[... gu₂]-⸢gal⸣ miṭ-ra-⸢at⸣ DINGIR.DINGIR ⸢li⸣-na-du šal-šiš

They shall praise him thirdly as [...], irrigation controller of the gods' canals,

o 8'8'

[... ḪE₂.GAL₂]⸢li⸣ ṭuḫ-di iš-pik!(ḪU-)ki GAL-MEŠ : ša₂-⸢kin⸣ meš-re-e mu-na-aḫ-ḫi-iš ⸢da-ad⸣-me

[...] of plenty, abundance, great stores (of crops) : provider of riches, the one who grants the inhabited world prosperity.

o 9'9'

[... šu-ʾa]-a mu-šab-šu-u₂ aš₂-na-an : dḪE₂.GAL₂ ⸢mu-kam⸣-mir ḪE₂.⸢GAL₂li ana ni-ši re⸣-biš ⸢liq⸣-[bu-u]

[...] of sheep, who called grain into existence : They shall call him fourthly HENGALLU, the one who piles up plenty for the people,

o 10'10'

[...] ḪE₂.NUN-šu₂ UGU KIti₃ <DAGALti₃> mu-deš-šu-⸢u₂ ur-qi₂⸣-[ti₃]

[...] his abundance over the extensive earth, who supplies vegetation copiously.

o 11'11'

[... ša]-⸢di-i⸣ e-⸢le-nu-uš⸣ ti-amat ša₂-⸢lil ša⸣-<lam>-⸢ta ta-a₃-wa-ti⸣ ina ⸢giš⸣[TUKUL-šu]

[...] a mountain above Tiamat, who plundered Tiamat's corpse with [his weapon],

o 12'12'

[...] ma-a-ti re-ʾu-u₂-ši-na ki-[i-na]

[...] of the land, their true shepherd,

o 13'13'

[... me₂]-⸢re⸣-šu₂ ⸢šu⸣-ku-us-su ši-⸢ir-ʾu⸣-[u?]

[...] is cultivated land, whose adornment is a furrow,

o 14'14'

[... i-ti]-⸢ib⸣-bi-ru ⸢uz-zu-uš-šu₂⸣

[...] crossed [...] continually in his anger,

o 15'15'

[... i-ti-it-ti]-⸢qu a-šar⸣ ša₂-⸢aš₂⸣-mi-ša₂

[...] constantly crossed the place of combat with her [...].

o 16'16'

[... ki]-⸢a-am⸣ : ⸢ti⸣-amat ru-⸢kub-šu-ma⸣ šu-u₂ ⸢ma-laḫ₃⸣-ša₂

[...] thus : Tiamat was her boat and he was her sailor.

o 17'17'

[... muš-tap]-⸢pi⸣-ik ka-re-⸢e⸣ [ti]-⸢li⸣ bit-⸢ru-ti⸣

[...] who constantly piles up grain-heaps (in) enormous mounds,

o 18'18'

[ba-nu]-⸢u₂ aš₂⸣-na-an u₃ U₈ ⸢SUM⸣nu NUMUN ma-a-⸢ti₃⸣

creator of grain and flocks, giver of the land's seed.

o 19'19'

⸢d⸣<<A>>.GILIM.MA mu-kin ṭur-ri DINGIR ba-nu-u₂ ki-na-a-[ti]1

GILIMA, establisher of the binding of the gods, creator of stability,

o 20'20'

rap-pu la-ʾi-iṭ-su-nu ⸢mu⸣-šaṣ-bi-tu₄ ⸢dam-qa⸣-a-[ti]

the hoop that confined them, (yet also) provider of good fortune.

o 21'21'

d<A>.GILIM.MA ša₂-qu-u₂ na-si-iḫ a-gi-i ⸢a-šir₃ šal-gi⸣

AGILIMA, the elevated one, remover of the tiara, inspector of snow,

o 22'22'

⸢ba⸣-nu-u KIti₃ e-⸢liš A-MEŠ⸣ mu-kin e-la-a-ti : dZU.LUM mu-⸢ad⸣-di qer-be₂-ti ana DINGIR.DINGIR ⸢pa-lik bi⸣-nu-ti

creator of the earth above the water, establisher of the upper parts : ZULUM, who assigns meadows for the gods, divider of creation,

o 23'23'

[na]-⸢din⸣ is-qi₂ u nin-da-be₂-e pa-qi₂-du eš-re-ti

giver of shares and food offerings, carer of chapels.

o 24'24'

[dmu]-⸢um⸣-mu ba-an ANe u KIti₃ mu-še-šer₃ DAG⸢si⸣

Mummu, creator of heaven and Underworld, who makes ... prosper,

o 25'25'

[DINGIR mu]-lil ANe u KIti₃ ša₂-niš dZU.LUM.UM.⸢MU⸣

[the god] who purifies heaven and Underworld, is secondly Zulummu,

Reverse
r 1r 1

[ša₂] ⸢a⸣-na dun-ni-šu i-na DINGIR.DINGIR ša₂-[nu]-u₂ la [...]

on account of whose power none other among the gods [...].

r 22

dGIŠ.NUMUN.AB₂ ba-nu-u₂ nap-ḫar UN-MEŠ e-pi-[šu₂] kib-ra-a-[ti]

GIŠNUMUNAB, creator of all the people, builder of the (four) edges,

r 33

⸢a⸣-bit DINGIR-MEŠ ša₂ ta-a₃-wa-ti e-piš UN-MEŠ ina mi-im-me-[šu₂-un]

destroyer of Tiamat's gods, maker of people from their parts.

r 44

[d]LUGAL.AB₂.DU₁₀.BUR₃ LUGAL sa-pi-iḫ ep-šet ti-amat na-⸢si⸣-ḫu gišTUKUL-[ša₂]

LUGALABDUBUR, the king, who scattered Tiamat's deeds (and) removed her weapon,

r 55

[ša₂ ina] ⸢re-ši u₃⸣ ar-ka-ti du-ru-us-⸢su⸣ kun-nu : dPA₄.GAL.GU₂.EN.NA a-ša₂-red nap-ḫar be-li ša₂ ša₂-qa e-⸢mu-qa-šu₂⸣

whose foundation is firm [in] front and rear : PAGALGUENNA, pre-eminent one of all the lords, whose strength is sublime,

r 66

[ša₂ ina DINGIR].⸢DINGIR ŠEŠ⸣-MEŠ-šu₂ šur-bu-u e-tel nap-ḫar-šu₂-un : dLUGAL.DUR.MAḪ LUGAL mar-kas₅ DINGIR.DINGIR be-el dur-ma-ḫi

who is the greatest among the gods, his brothers, lord of all of them : LUGALDURMAH, king of the bond of the gods, lord of the cosmic rope,

r 77

[ša₂] ⸢i⸣-na šu-bat ⸢šar⸣-ru-ti šur-bu-u₂ i-na DINGIR.DINGIR ma-aʾ-diš ṣi-⸢ru⸣

[who] is the greatest in the royal residence, most exalted among the gods.

r 88

[d]⸢A⸣.RA₂.NUN.NA ma-⸢lik d⸣e₂-a ba-an DINGIR.DINGIR ⸢AD⸣-MEŠ-[šu]

ARANUNA, adviser of Ea, creator of the gods, [his] fathers,

r 99

[ša₂ a]-⸢na a-lak⸣-ti ru-bu-ti-⸢šu₂ la u₂⸣-maš-ša₂-lu ⸢DINGIR a⸣-a-⸢um-ma⸣

whose way of rulership no god can equal.

r 1010

[d]⸢DUMU⸣::DU₆.:KU₃ ša₂ ina ⸢DU₆⸣.KU₃ u₂-ta-⸢ad-da-šu šu⸣-bat-su [el]-⸢let⸣

DUMUDUKU, who renews for himself his pure dwelling in Duku1,

r 1111

[d]⸢DUMU⸣.DU₆.KU₃ ša₂ ina ba-li-šu₂ ⸢EŠ.BAR la⸣ KU₅su dLUGAL.DU₆.KU₃

Dumuduku, without whom Lugalduku2 does not decide a verdict.

r 1212

[dLUGAL.ŠU.AN].⸢NA⸣ LUGAL ša₂ ina ⸢DINGIR.DINGIR⸣ [... e]-⸢mu-qa⸣-šu : be-lu₄ ⸢e-muq⸣ GAN ZE₂ SIG? šu-⸢tu⸣-[ru ...]

LUGALŠUANNA, the king whose strength is [...] among the gods : the lord, strength of ... supreme [...]

r 1313

[dIR].⸢UG₇?⸣.GI ⸢ša₂-lil gim-ri-šu₂-nu qer-biš ti-amat⸣ [:] ⸢ša₂⸣ nap-ḫar uz-ni iḫ-mu-mu ḫa-⸢si⸣-[sa] ⸢pal⸣-ki

IRUGGA, plunderer of them all inside the sea [:] who gathered all intelligence (and) is of wide wisdom.

r 1414

[dIR].QIN.GU ša₂-lil dqin-gu a-a-bi-iš ta-ḫa-zi ⸢: mut⸣-[tab]-⸢bil te-ret nap⸣-ḫa-⸢ri mu⸣-kin be-lu-ti

IRQINGU, who plundered Qingu like an enemy in a battle : organizer of all instructions, establisher of lordship.

r 1515

[dKIN].⸢MA⸣ mu-ma-ʾe-er nap-ḫar DINGIR.DINGIR na-⸢din⸣ [mil]-ki

KINMA, commander of all the gods, giver of advice,

r 1616

[ša₂ a-na] šu-me-šu DINGIR.DINGIR ki-ma me-ḫe-e i-šub-bu ⸢pal⸣-ḫiš

[at whose] name the gods tremble fearfully like (a reed in) a storm wind.

r 1717

[d]⸢E₂⸣.SISKUR₂ ša₂-qiš i-na <E₂> ik-ri-bi li-šib-ma

ESISKUR shall sit high up in the house of prayer and

r 1818

[DINGIR.DINGIR] ⸢maḫ⸣-ru-uš-šu li-še-ri-bu kad₃-ra-šu₂-un : a-di i-rib-šu₂-nu i-maḫ-⸢ḫa⸣-ru-ni

[the gods] shall bring in their presents before him : until he receives their gifts.

r 1919

[mam-ma]-⸢an⸣ DINGIR.DINGIR la i-ban-na-a nik-la-a-ti : ⸢er-ba ṣal⸣-mat SAG.DU bi-⸢na⸣-tuš-šu₂

No one (else) of the gods creates artful things : the four (regions) of black-headed people are his creation,

r 2020

[e-la ša₂-a]-⸢šu⸣ ṭe₃-mi <u₄-me>-ši-na la ia-a-ad-<da> DINGIR ma-am-man : dGIRA₃ mu-⸢kin a-ṣa⸣-at gišTUKUL

[Apart from] him, none of the gods knows the plan of their days : GIRU, establisher of the projection of a weapon,

r 2121

[ša₂ ina ME₃] ⸢ti⸣-amat i-ban-na-a nik-la-a-ti : pal-ka uz-ni it-pe-šu ḫa-si-sa

[who] created artful things [in the battle] with Tiamat : (who) is wide in intelligence, competent in wisdom,

r 2222

[lib-bu ru]-⸢qu⸣ ša₂ la i-lam-ma-du DINGIR.DINGIR gim-ras-[su-un]

a deep [heart] that the gods in their totality do not understand.

r 2323

[dAD.DU lu] ⸢MU-šu⸣ kiš-šat ANe li-rim-[ma]

[ADDU shall be] his name, he shall cover the whole of heaven,

r 2424

[... KI]⸢ti₃ li⸣-ir-ta-aṣ-ṣi-[in]

[...] shall rumble loudly [over the earth],

r 2525

[... liš-tak]-⸢ṣi⸣-ba-⸢am⸣-[ma]

[... shall] reach fullness [and]

(rest of reverse missing)

1{d}A.GILIM.MA and {d}GILIM.MA (l.21') seem to be interchanged in this manuscript.

1 DU₆.KU , meaning "pure mound ", is another name for Apsu.

2 Lugalduku refers to Ea.

Created by Greta Van Buylaere for the AHRC-funded GKAB Project, 2010 and released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/cams/gkab/P338327
